g.data-narration: Menus are essential for professional desktop applications, providing organized access to functionality.
Menu-driven interfaces organize application commands intuitively, following platform conventions for accessibility.
g.text: Desktop applications use menus for organized command access
Java Swing provides: MenuBar, Menu, MenuItem, CheckboxMenuItem, PopupMenu

g.data-narration: The menu hierarchy flows from MenuBar to Menu, then to MenuItem and CheckboxMenuItem components.
MenuBar is the container for menus, added to Frame using setMenuBar() with only one per frame allowed.
g.text: Container for Menu objects • Frame can have at most ONE MenuBar
Added to Frame via setMenuBar() method

g.data-narration: Menu represents pull-down menus containing MenuItems, can be nested as submenus, with optional separator lines.
g.text: Pull-down menu displayed from menu bar • Can contain submenus
📁 Submenu: Menu can contain other Menu objects for cascading menus

g.data-narration: MenuItem is the basic selectable item generating ActionEvent when clicked, supporting shortcuts and enable/disable.
g.text: Basic building block of menus • Generates ActionEvent when selected
⚡ Event: Uses ActionListener for handling selections

g.data-narration: CheckboxMenuItem provides toggle states with checkmarks, generating ItemEvent for binary options like Bold or Italic.
g.text: Toggle menu items with on/off states • Displays checkmark when selected
⚡ Event: Uses ItemListener (NOT ActionListener) for state changes

g.data-narration: PopupMenu appears at specific coordinates on right-click, requiring MouseListener with isPopupTrigger() checks.
g.text: Context menu displayed at specific location • Triggered by right-click

g.data-narration: Mnemonics are Alt+key shortcuts shown as underlined characters, while accelerators work globally with Ctrl combinations.
g.text: Underlined character • Activated with Alt key
Global shortcuts • Work regardless of focus

g.data-narration: Different menu components use different listeners: ActionListener for MenuItem, ItemListener for CheckboxMenuItem.
A basic menu demo creates MenuBar, adds File and Edit menus with MenuItems, and handles actions via ActionListener.
Advanced menus include mnemonics, accelerators, CheckboxMenuItems for font styling with ItemListener.
PopupMenu requires MouseListener checking isPopupTrigger() in both mousePressed and mouseReleased for cross-platform compatibility.
AWT uses MenuBar/Menu/MenuItem while Swing uses JMenuBar/JMenu/JMenuItem with setJMenuBar() method.
Remember key methods: add(), addSeparator(), setMnemonic(), setAccelerator(), getState(), show() for PopupMenu.
Avoid mistakes: using AWT with JFrame, forgetting listeners, checking popup trigger only once, confusing mnemonics with accelerators.
Master menus by understanding the hierarchy, event types, shortcuts, and AWT-Swing differences for professional GUI development.
